title: Infosys Clarifies DGGI Show Cause Notice on Alleged Ineligible GST Refunds
detailed_summary: Infosys clarified media reports dated 8 th  October 2025 about a Directorate General of GST Intelligence show cause notice relating to alleged ineligible ITC refunds. DGGI had sought information in May 2025, issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, and then issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties. The notice alleges that services provided by Infosys’s overseas branches are not export of services and that GST refunds claimed by the Company were erroneous. Infosys said it assessed the notice, obtained external tax and legal advice, and filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the notice. The Company stated that there is no tax demand as on date and no material impact from the article.

key_facts.fact: Media reports published on 8 th  October 2025 referred to a show cause notice issued by Directorate General of GST Intelligence for Rs 415 crore to Infosys over alleged ineligible ITC refunds.
DGGI sought information in May 2025 on GST refunds claimed by Infosys, and Infosys provided relevant information and met with DGGI officials.
DGGI issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, after which Infosys sought additional information, relevant documents, and time to file a response.
DGGI issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
The show cause notice alleges that services provided by Infosys’s overseas branches are not export of services and that the refund claimed by the Company is erroneous.
Infosys filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
Infosys stated that there is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

litigation_or_regulatory_matters.description: DGGI issued a show cause notice alleging that services provided by Infosys’s overseas branches are not export of services and that GST refunds claimed by the Company are erroneous. Infosys has filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.amount: Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.status: There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

risk_factors.risk: DGGI has issued a show cause notice alleging erroneous GST refund claims related to services provided by Infosys’s overseas branches.
risk_factors.potential_impact: Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties

important_text_spans.text: DGGI did not provide the additional time requested and instead issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the amount, date, and nature of the regulatory tax notice.

important_text_spans.text: As per the show cause notice from DGGI, the services provided by the company's overseas branches are not export of services and hence the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
important_text_spans.reason: Explains the core allegation in the tax dispute.

important_text_spans.text: Pursuant to the advice, the Company has on 19 th  September 2025, filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports Infosys’s stated legal response and current procedural status.

important_text_spans.text: There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports Infosys’s statement on current demand status.
